rationale: Within a ~4-second window, corp\Administrator executed five distinct domain user-enumeration commands from C:\Temp\ via a cmd.exe session: 'net user /domain' (A0), 'net users /domain' (EV-001), 'dsquery user' (EV-002), 'wmic ... PATH ds_user GET ds_samaccountname' (EV-003), 'Get-WMIObject -class ds_user' (EV-004), and 'Get-ADUser -Filter *' (EV-005). The rapid, sequential use of multiple redundant techniques to enumerate all domain users — spanning net.exe, dsquery, WMIC/LDAP, and two PowerShell AD methods — is strongly indicative of automated or scripted Active Directory reconnaissance (T1087.002), not routine administration. The working directory C:\Temp\ is atypical for legitimate admin activity, and the parent cmd.exe was launched with 'pushd C:\Temp', suggesting a staged toolset. No benign explanation accounts for five overlapping enumeration methods executed in under 4 seconds; however, no lateral movement, credential dumping, or exfiltration evidence is present in this package, capping confidence below critical.
